question: Does the mega-commit RMSNormBackward/LayerNormBackward ~2x regression (395->817us) PERSIST at HEAD?
answer: HEALED — does NOT ship. Directly measured at HEAD daa79cd25ca: RMSNormBackward/000=306.9us, LayerNormBackward/000=307.0us (mega had 817; BASE 395/392). HEAD is faster than BASE on both.
heal_location: between mega-commit 97385fb3273 and a85d79a900a (scalar-acc predecessor). norm-bwd/000 trajectory: BASE 395 -> mega 817 -> a85d79a900a 307 -> stays ~307 through HEAD (all 4 mover pairs).
mechanism: Regressed kernel = the second reduction in RMSNorm/LayerNorm backward fusion (triton_*_mul_sum_1, the weight-gradient dw reduction over the token dim). MEGA emitted it as a LOOPED reduction (triton_red_fused_..._sum_1 @triton_heuristics.reduction, R0_BLOCK multi-pass) = ~2x slower for this r0-resident shape. BASE and HEAD emit it as a SINGLE-PASS triton_per_fused_..._sum_1 @triton_heuristics.persistent_reduction. The MixOrderReduction split kernel (sum_0, fixed_config RSPLIT_SIZE=128, mor_finalize) is BIT-IDENTICAL between mega and HEAD and is NOT the regression. HEAD beats BASE because HEAD turns ALL of these reductions persistent (0 looped reduction kernels vs BASE's 2), shaving a further ~22% off norm-bwd.
ship_status: NO shipped regression. The 817us is an artifact of benching the SQUASHED mega-commit in isolation; that synthetic aggregate state never appears as a real branch HEAD.
